planning_summary: The azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting.web_app resource is fully covered by the avm-res-web-site module via its diagnostic_settings input. All relevant attributes (workspace, enabled_log/categories, metrics, retention policies) can be mapped to the diagnostic_settings structure in the AVM module. No output mapping is needed as this resource is an attached subresource. No additional variables are required.
transformation_description: The azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting.web_app resource will be migrated into the diagnostic_settings map attribute of the avm-res-web-site module. The original azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting resource is removed, and all properties will be transferred to the AVM module configuration for diagnostic settings.

attribute_mappings.handling_reason: The diagnostic setting name directly maps to the AVM diagnostic_settings map key or 'name' attribute.
Workspace resource ID directly maps to workspace_resource_id in AVM.
AVM expects a set of log category strings; source resource provides repeated blocks of 'enabled_log' with 'category' attributes. Format transformation needed to flatten.
attribute_mappings.format_transformation_description: Aggregate each enabled_log block's category into a single list/set for AVM.

attribute_mappings.handling_reason: Source metric block(s) provide 'category' which can be mapped to AVM set of strings.
attribute_mappings.format_transformation_description: Extract metric 'category' value from metric blocks and flatten to set/list.

attribute_mappings.handling_reason: AVM module expects log_groups, not present in the original resource. Default value matches AVM's default.
attribute_mappings.additional_notes: Can use AVM default ['allLogs'] if not specified.

attribute_mappings.handling_reason: Retention in days not specified for logs/metrics in AVM; original resource metrics retention_policy.enabled = false, so use default. Explicit mapping not required unless user customization needed.
attribute_mappings.additional_notes: Will inherit AVM defaults unless overridden.

risk_notes: Mapping is direct and supported by the AVM module. Risk is low due to limited customization and clear attribute alignment. Some AVM options like log_groups and retention_in_days may require user awareness of defaults.
